title: Dortmanna cordigera
intro: Dortmanna cordigera, also known as Sweet goldenrod or Yellow goldenrod, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. This plant is valued for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which can aid in soothing digestive issues, relieving menstrual cramps, and reducing fever. The bioactive constituents of Dortmanna cordigera include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Dortmanna cordigera is commonly prepared in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as used in topical applications to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Dortmanna cordigera is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Alismataceae. It belongs to the genus Dortmanna, which is a group of aquatic plants. Dortmanna cordigera is native to Europe and Western Asia and is often found in freshwater habitats. The plant is a perennial aquatic species, characterized by its distinctive leaves and white flowers. Within the kingdom Plantae, Dortmanna cordigera is further classified as a member of the class Liliopsida and order Alismatales.
